question: Do the eight PRESENT r3 true ciphertext output positions frozen on seed0 remain predictable under a second independent fixed secret key, and does an eight-output head reduce full-output multitask interference?
cross_key_gate: At least four of the eight preregistered positions pass all three per-bit gates.
dedicated_head_gate: Mean selected8 AUC minus mean full64 AUC on the same eight positions is at least 0.002.
next_action_on_pass: Advance the same positions and the stronger frozen anchor to a preregistered PRESENT r4 selected-bit and structured-XOR comparison without reselection.
stop_action: If fewer than four positions confirm, stop seed, epoch, data, and round scaling and audit seed0 key conditioning.
